title: Helanthium nymphaeifolium
taxonomy: Helanthium nymphaeifolium is classified in the family Hydrocharitaceae, commonly known as the waterweeds or tapegrass family. It is a genus of aquatic plants native to North America. This genus is closely related to the genus Egeria, both of which are aquatic plants with similar characteristics. Helanthium nymphaeifolium is further classified as a species within the genus Helanthium. It is considered a type of flowering plant that grows in freshwater environments.
uses: Helanthium nymphaeifolium, also known as the zebra's tail or dwarf waterplantain, has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties.
